target.title: fix(agents): publish owned announcement session writes
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?
- Fixes a session-lock publication gap where prompt-submission owned writes could lose the `publishOwnedWrite` option before reaching the session lock controller.
- This lets legitimate same-process announcement/completion writes be recognized by the original attempt fence while keeping external edit takeover protection intact.

Why does this matter now?
- #88703 reports `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` after a yielded media completion falls back to an announce run that writes the same requester session.

What is the intended outcome?
- The prompt-lock wrapper forwards write-lock options, so owned transcript-write publication reaches the fence logic.

What is intentionally out of scope?
- This does not implement a broader yielded-run resume/wake mechanism.
- This does not relax takeover detection for unowned external edits.

What does success look like?
- Same-process announcement writes published through the prompt context are accepted on reacquire; plain external edits still throw `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`.

What should reviewers focus on?
- Whether option forwarding is limited to the intended prompt-lock wrapper and whether the regression preserves the external-edit guard.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #88703

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related issue comments on #88703 report adjacent non-media cron failures with the same exception, but this PR targets the source-repro media/announcement publication path from the issue review.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request observed; this follows the ClawSweeper-reviewed source-repro issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: owned announcement writes during the prompt lock release window were not published to the original session fence because the wrapper dropped `publishOwnedWrite`.
- Real environment tested: Local development checkout on branch `fix/announce-owned-write-88703`, rebased on `upstream/main` at `92b9cd21ec`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a local runtime proof script with `node --import tsx` that creates real temp session transcript state, installs `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease`, simulates a yielded media-completion announcement through `runWithOwnedSessionTranscriptWritePublication`, then checks the requester session fence.
  - Re-ran focused checks: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-prerelease-test-plan.test.ts`, `pnpm check:test-types`, and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "forwardedOptions": [
    {
      "publishOwnedWrite": true
    }
  ],
  "postAnnouncementResult": "accepted",
  "takeoverAfterOwnedAnnouncement": false,
  "externalEditRejected": true,
  "takeoverAfterExternalEdit": true,
  "transcriptLines": [
    "{"type":"session"}",
    "{"type":"message","id":"media-completion-announced"}",
    "{"type":"message","id":"post-announcement"}",
    "{"type":"message","id":"external-after-announcement"}"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the prompt wrapper forwarded `{ publishOwnedWrite: true }`, the original requester controller accepted the post-announcement write with no takeover after the owned completion announcement, and a later unowned external edit was still rejected as `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live image-generation provider completion against external provider credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof exercises the actual local prompt-wrapper/session-fence code path with a simulated media-completion transcript write; live provider image generation would require configured media provider credentials and a longer end-to-end channel run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper review on #88703 identified the prompt submission wrapper as dropping the owned-write publication option before it reached the session lock controller.


## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`

`git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

`attempt.session-lock.test.ts` now covers a prompt-stream announcement write from another controller that is published and accepted, followed by an unowned external edit that is still rejected.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The prompt submission wrapper passed only the operation into `withSessionWriteLock`, dropping the options object that carries `publishOwnedWrite`.

If no test was added, why not?

A regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The session takeover fence, because it protects against real external edits.

How is that risk mitigated?

The fix only forwards existing options, and the regression explicitly verifies that a later unowned external edit still throws takeover protection.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and optional maintainer/live media-completion confirmation.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial implementation for #88703.

target.title: feat(agents): generalized native compaction ownership for CLI backends
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ownsNativeCompaction` capability to `CliBackendPlugin` so backends that manage their own transcript compaction can declare it and OpenClaw defers instead of fighting or hard-failing the turn
- Claude-cli is the first adopter: Claude Code compacts its own transcript internally, so OpenClaw returns a no-op from the compaction path
- Codex's existing native-harness compaction path is unchanged (agentHarnessId match still routes to the harness endpoint)
- Generalizes the partial fix in #87785 (codex-scoped) to a capability any CLI backend can opt into

## Problem

`claude-cli` runs Claude Code, which owns its own transcript and auto-compacts when context fills. OpenClaw does not know this, so the safeguard summarizer fires and fights Claude's compaction, hard-failing the turn.

The current workaround is `contextTokens: 1_000_000` so the safeguard never fires. This PR removes the need for that workaround.

## Behavior proof

Production compaction failure captured from a live claude-cli session (session `39bd8759`, 2026-05-16T21:59:57Z):

```
[assistant] [provider: openclaw, model: delivery-mirror]
Context limit exceeded during compaction. I've reset our conversation
to start fresh - please try again.

To prevent this, increase your compaction buffer by setting
`agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor` to 20000 or higher
in your config.
```

This is the exact failure mode this PR prevents: OpenClaw's safeguard summarizer fires on a claude-cli session that already manages its own compaction, exceeds its budget fighting the backend's own compaction lifecycle, and force-resets the conversation. With `ownsNativeCompaction: true`, the lifecycle returns a no-op and the session continues normally.

The workaround currently deployed (`contextTokens: 1_000_000`) masks the issue by preventing the safeguard from ever firing. This PR fixes the root cause.

## Changes

**`src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts`** -- `ownsNativeCompaction?: boolean` on `CliBackendPlugin`

**`src/agents/cli-backends.ts`** -- Propagated through `ResolvedCliBackend`, `FallbackCliBackendPolicy`, and all 4 resolution paths

**`src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts`** -- Added `resolveCliBackendConfig` to injectable deps. In `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle`, when `ownsNativeCompaction` is true and no native harness endpoint exists, returns a no-op early

**`extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts`** -- `ownsNativeCompaction: true` on claude-cli

## Scope

Only changes behavior for CLI backends that explicitly opt in via `ownsNativeCompaction: true`. Every other backend keeps the existing safeguard. Does not touch the embedded runner path.

## Not tested

- acpx / copilot backends (candidates for future adoption, not wired in this PR)
- Full e2e filling a context window with the fix applied (validated via unit tests covering the exact code path + production failure evidence above)

## Risk / mitigation

- **Risk:** A backend incorrectly declares ownership but does not actually compact -- context grows unbounded. **Mitigation:** Opt-in flag, not default; only set on claude-cli which is known to own compaction.
- **Risk:** Breaks existing codex path. **Mitigation:** `ownsNativeCompaction` early-out only fires when `isNativeHarnessCompactionSession` returns false; codex sessions with `agentHarnessId` still route to the existing native harness path. Verified by test.

## Test plan

- [x] Backend with `ownsNativeCompaction` and no harness endpoint skips compaction (no-op)
- [x] Backend without `ownsNativeCompaction` compacts normally via context engine
- [x] Backend with `ownsNativeCompaction` AND agentHarnessId uses existing native harness path
- [x] `resolveCliBackendConfig("claude-cli")` returns `ownsNativeCompaction: true`
- [x] All 30 existing cli-compaction tests pass (no regressions)
- [x] All 48 existing cli-backends tests pass (no regressions)


---

## Over-budget reproduction (review follow-up)

The defer is proven deterministically on the real code path (see **Test plan**): without the flag OpenClaw compaction runs (`cli-compaction.test.ts:1139`); with the flag and no harness endpoint OpenClaw defers to the backend (`cli-compaction.test.ts:1071`).

```
npx vitest run src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts -t "ownsNativeCompaction"
ŌåÆ Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 6 passed | 24 skipped (30)
```

A redacted **live** over-budget Claude CLI capture could not be synthesized in a normal-sized-session environment, for a structural reason worth recording: claude-opus's effective context budget is the hardcoded GA 1M window (`context.ts` ŌåÆ `shouldUseDiscoveredAnthropicGa1MContextWindow`), and real sessions sit far below it (~130K), so the over-budget branch never triggers organically (`compactionCount = 0` across sessions). Forcing it via config does not work ŌĆö per-session `contextTokens` is re-derived from `agentMeta` (not durable); `models.providers.*.contextTokens` is overridden by the 1M GA hardcode; and `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokens` is not wired into the cli-compaction budget (set to 1,000,000 against a 127K-token session and the defer still did not fire). A genuine live capture therefore requires an organically >1M-token Claude CLI session.

target.title: [codex] Read exact X posts via FxTwitter
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a key-free FxTwitter exact-post reader for public X/Twitter status URLs and numeric status IDs.
- Route exact post references through FxTwitter before falling back to xAI-backed broader `x_search`.
- Keep generic X search on the existing xAI auth path and update docs/tests/build guards for the new behavior.

target.title: fix(openai): avoid stale Responses message id replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenAI Responses request replay can reuse a signed assistant `message` item id after the corresponding `reasoning` item has been pruned from local history.
- Solution: replay signed assistant message ids only when the immediately preceding replayed item is the paired reasoning item.
- What changed: added a small replay-id policy helper, wired it into the OpenAI Responses transport, and covered the pruned-reasoning case in the existing reasoning replay tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change reasoning-item replay, tool-call replay, payload metadata, model selection, or Telegram formatting.

## Motivation

This prevents a latent OpenAI Responses failure exposed by long-running sessions whose assistant text still has a persisted Responses item id but whose paired reasoning item is no longer replayable. In that state, the next request can be rejected before the model responds.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI Responses rejected replayed conversation history when an assistant `message` item id was included without the required paired `reasoning` item, while phase metadata still needs to survive without replaying orphan provider-owned assistant ids.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS arm64 using the OpenAI Responses transport path, plus a live OpenAI Responses API request against `gpt-5.5` on May 31, 2026 after maintainer fixup `d6902ed1a0f13c7b73e69fcfdc6566086b3c532f`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'INNER'
import OpenAI from 'openai';
import { buildOpenAIResponsesParams } from './src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts';

const model = {
  id: 'gpt-5.4',
  name: 'gpt-5.4',
  api: 'openai-responses',
  provider: 'openai',
  baseUrl: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  reasoning: true,
  input: ['text'],
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
  contextWindow: 128000,
  maxTokens: 4096,
};
const ZERO_USAGE = {
  input: 0,
  output: 0,
  cacheRead: 0,
  cacheWrite: 0,
  totalTokens: 0,
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0, total: 0 },
};
const context = {
  systemPrompt: 'system',
  messages: [
    { role: 'user', content: 'Earlier prompt', timestamp: 1 },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Earlier answer',
          textSignature: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'thinking',
          thinking: 'chain',
          thinkingSignature: JSON.stringify({
            id: 'rs_reasoning_item',
            type: 'reasoning',
            summary: [],
          }),
        },
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Pinned commentary answer',
          textSignature: JSON.stringify({ v: 1, id: 'msg_commentary', phase: 'commentary' }),
        },
      ],
    },
    { role: 'user', content: 'Latest prompt', timestamp: 3 },
  ],
};
const params = buildOpenAIResponsesParams(model, context, {});
const captured = {};
const client = new OpenAI({
  apiKey: 'test',
  baseURL: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  fetch: async (url, init) => {
    captured.url = String(url);
    captured.body = init?.body ? String(init.body) : '';
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({
      id: 'resp_test',
      object: 'response',
      created_at: 0,
      status: 'completed',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      output: [],
      parallel_tool_calls: false,
      tools: [],
    }), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' },
    });
  },
});
await client.responses.create({ ...params, stream: false });
const payload = JSON.stringify(params.input);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  proof: 'openai-responses-sdk-loopback',
  pr_head: 'b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60',
  request_path: new URL(captured.url).pathname,
  stale_signed_message_id: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
  stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  stale_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_commentary'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_commentary'),
}, null, 2));
INNER
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Before this patch, the live OpenClaw Telegram agent diagnosed a real OpenAI Responses failure after conversation pruning:

```text
OpenAI rejected the next request with:
message was provided without its required reasoning item

Failure shape:
- prior assistant message ids were replayed from textSignature
- paired reasoning items had been pruned/unavailable
```

After this patch, I reran the current-head OpenAI SDK loopback on `b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60`. It imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, sent that exact payload through OpenAI Node SDK 6.39.0 with an in-process `fetch` loopback, and confirmed that both the OpenClaw payload and the serialized SDK `/v1/responses` request omitted the stale raw signed assistant message id while preserving phase metadata and omitting the phase-tagged provider-owned replay id:

```json
{
  "proof": "openai-responses-sdk-loopback",
  "pr_head": "b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60",
  "request_path": "/v1/responses",
  "stale_signed_message_id": "msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning",
  "stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": false,
  "stale_id_present_in_sdk_request": false,
  "commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": true,
  "commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request": true
}
```

Maintainer live proof on `d6902ed1a0f13c7b73e69fcfdc6566086b3c532f` sent the PR-built payload to the real OpenAI Responses API. The setup produced both `reasoning` and `message` output items; the replayed message preserved `phase: final_answer`, omitted the provider-owned message id, and the live request completed.

```json
{
  "proof": "live-openai-responses-phase-id-omitted",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "setup_output_types": ["reasoning", "message"],
  "replayed_message_has_id": false,
  "replayed_message_phase": "final_answer",
  "live_result_status": "completed"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: stale raw signed assistant message ids are omitted when their required reasoning item is absent; phase-tagged provider ids are omitted without losing phase metadata, and unsigned assistant messages still get the existing synthetic fallback id.
- What was not tested: a live deployed gateway restart has not been run; focused replay tests now pass via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` (3 shards, 228 tests), alongside a live OpenAI Responses API proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live OpenClaw agent output above captured the OpenAI rejection text and identified the pruned-reasoning replay shape.


## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the Responses replay path treated persisted assistant text signatures as independently replayable message ids, but OpenAI can require the corresponding reasoning item to be replayed with that message item.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing coverage checked reasoning replay when reasoning was present; it did not check the pruned-reasoning case where only the signed assistant message survived.
- Contributing context (if known): long-lived sessions can prune or omit reasoning items while retaining assistant text signatures.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a signed assistant message id is omitted when no paired reasoning item was replayed; the same signed id is preserved when paired reasoning was replayed; unsigned text still uses the fallback id.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in request payload construction, before network IO, so the replay-id policy can be tested deterministically.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for the pruned-reasoning signed-message case.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None, except affected OpenAI Responses sessions avoid a provider-side 400 when replay history has a stale signed message id without its paired reasoning item.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request replays message id] -> [OpenAI rejects]

After:
[assistant textSignature id survives] -> [reasoning item pruned] -> [request omits stale message id] -> [request remains valid]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS arm64
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest via OpenClaw source checkout
- Model/provider: `openai/gpt-5.4`, OpenAI Responses
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram agent session exposed the failure; source regression covers the transport replay policy.
- Relevant config (redacted): OpenAI Responses API model with reasoning enabled.

### Steps

1. Create a replay candidate with a signed assistant text `message` id.
2. Omit the paired reasoning item, matching a pruned history state.
3. Build the next OpenAI Responses request.

### Expected

- The stale signed assistant message id is omitted when paired reasoning is unavailable.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale signed assistant message id was replayed even when the paired reasoning item was unavailable.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: live OpenAI Responses `gpt-5.5` API proof via an inline `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script that imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`; focused replay regression coverage via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-responses.reasoning-replay.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts` (3 shards, 228 tests); `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files; `git diff --check`; autoreview clean.
- Edge cases checked: raw and phase-tagged signed ids without reasoning are omitted; phase metadata is preserved; signed id with reasoning is preserved; unsigned assistant text keeps the synthetic fallback id.
- What you did **not** verify: live gateway deployment on this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: omitting a stale Responses message id may reduce provider-side continuity for that one historical assistant message.
  - Mitigation: the text content and phase metadata are still replayed; the id is omitted only when the paired reasoning item is missing, which is the provider-rejected shape.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent:turn:end and agent:turn:save internal hook events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP turn completion updates latency/error stats, but there was no internal hook surface that distinguished "the runtime turn ended" from "the turn save phase reached an outcome."
- Why it matters: Existing signals are per-message, sub-turn, or session-level. Post-turn automation such as memory, indexing, audit, and analytics need a deterministic turn boundary, and some consumers specifically need to know whether durable session state is readable.
- What changed: Added two narrow ACP turn internal hook events:
  - `agent:turn:end`: lifecycle boundary after ACP terminal event delivery, emitted on success and failure.
  - `agent:turn:save`: persistence outcome boundary after the ACP turn save phase resolves; its `success` field describes save success/failure and `turnSuccess` preserves the runtime turn outcome.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add `agent:turn:start`, new config, user-facing messaging changes, `CHANGELOG.md` edits, or broader lifecycle refactors.
- Review context: This keeps the #37833 lineage and implements ClawSweeper's "split timing semantics before merge" option without making a truthy event name carry failure semantics.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #37833
- Related #60734
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Internal hook consumers can subscribe to two ACP turn lifecycle events:

| Event | Fires when | Durable session-state guarantee |
|---|---|---|
| `agent:turn:end` | The ACP runtime turn completes or fails after terminal event delivery | No |
| `agent:turn:save` | The ACP turn save phase reaches an outcome after the dispatch transcript save step runs or declines | Only when `context.success === true` |

`agent:turn:end` carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode` for runtime turn failures.

`agent:turn:save` carries `sessionKey`, `success` for the save outcome, `turnSuccess` for the underlying runtime turn outcome, `durationMs`, optional `turnErrorCode`, and optional `saveError` when the save callback throws.

If saving the mirrored dispatch turn fails, `agent:turn:end` still fires and `agent:turn:save` fires with `success: false`. Hook handlers are bounded fire-and-forget and are not awaited through later ACP state transitions or runtime cleanup.

No user-facing messaging or config behavior changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ACP turn completes] -> [latency/error stats only]

After:
[ACP turn completes]
  -> [latency/error stats]
  -> [agent:turn:end internal hook]
  -> [save mirrored ACP dispatch turn]
  -> [agent:turn:save internal hook]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP manager with an in-process test runtime backend
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated test config/state

### Steps

1. Register internal hook listeners for `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:save`.
2. Trigger a successful ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
3. Trigger a failing ACP turn through `AcpSessionManager`.
4. Trigger a successful ACP turn whose save step fails.
5. Trigger ACP dispatch and verify transcript persistence runs before successful `agent:turn:save`.

### Expected

- Successful saves emit `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:save` with `success: true` on both events.
- Failed turns emit `agent:turn:end` with `success: false` and `errorCode`.
- Failed turns with an ACP dispatch save callback emit `agent:turn:save` with `success: false`, `turnSuccess: false`, and `turnErrorCode`.
- Save failures do not suppress `agent:turn:end`; `agent:turn:save` fires with `success: false` and `saveError`.
- Dispatch-backed ACP turns persist transcript output before successful `agent:turn:save` can report `success: true`.
- Hook listener failures do not break the turn lifecycle.

### Actual

- Added manager-level tests covering successful, failed, saved, save-ineligible, and save-failure turns.
- Added ACP dispatch tests covering transcript persistence before successful `agent:turn:save` and persistence failure being reported as a failed save outcome.
- Added internal-hook type guard coverage for both hook events.
- Updated hook docs to document the split timing contract.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ACP turn hooks now expose two distinct internal boundaries: runtime turn completion via `agent:turn:end` and persistence outcome via `agent:turn:save`.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at `1f00685e57`, running production `AcpSessionManager` and the production internal hook registry with an in-process ACP runtime backend.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD) pnpm exec tsx ./tmp-pr-71399-hook-proof.ts`. The temporary proof harness registered real internal hook listeners, drove `AcpSessionManager.runTurn()` through save success, runtime failure with save declined, save callback failure, and save-declined paths, then asserted the emitted hooks.
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal console output from the local proof run:

```text
PR #71399 hook proof
head: 1f00685e57
environment: local OpenClaw source checkout; production AcpSessionManager and internal hook registry; in-process ACP runtime backend

case: success-save
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:text_delta -> event:done -> save:ok -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=true, turnSuccess=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}

case: runtime-failure-save-declined
  rejectedCode: ACP_TURN_FAILED
  order: event:error -> save:false -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=false, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=false, turnSuccess=false, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED, saveError=none}

case: save-failure
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:done -> save:throw -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=false, turnSuccess=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=durable transcript write failed}

case: save-returned-false
  rejectedCode: none
  order: event:done -> save:false -> hook:agent:turn:end -> hook:agent:turn:save
  hooks: agent:turn:end{success=true, turnSuccess=none, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}, agent:turn:save{success=false, turnSuccess=true, durationMs=number, errorCode=none, turnErrorCode=none, saveError=none}

assertions: passed
```

- **Observed result after fix**: runtime success with durable save produced both hooks with successful outcomes; runtime failure produced `agent:turn:end` with `success=false` and `errorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED` plus `agent:turn:save` with `success=false`, `turnSuccess=false`, and `turnErrorCode=ACP_TURN_FAILED`; save callback failure produced `agent:turn:save` with `success=false`, `turnSuccess=true`, and `saveError`.
- **What was not tested**: a networked external `acpx` adapter process; this proof exercises OpenClaw's ACP manager and internal hook registry with an in-process runtime backend.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `agent:turn:end` type guard recognizes the event shape, including optional `errorCode`
  - `agent:turn:save` type guard recognizes the event shape, including `turnSuccess`, optional `turnErrorCode`, and optional `saveError`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:end` after successful turns
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:save` after successful save turns
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:end` after failed turns with `errorCode: "ACP_TURN_FAILED"`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:save` when the save callback fails, carrying `success: false` and `saveError`
  - ACP manager emits `agent:turn:save` when the save callback returns `false`, carrying `success: false`
  - terminal ACP events are delivered before `agent:turn:end`
  - ACP dispatch persists transcript output before successful `agent:turn:save`
  - hook dispatch uses `fireAndForgetBoundedHook(() => triggerInternalHook(...))`
  - docs describe the separate lifecycle and durable-state semantics
- Commands run locally for this revision:
  - `pnpm test src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `git diff --check`
- What I did **not** verify:
  - a networked external `acpx` adapter process; this PR verifies the OpenClaw ACP manager, dispatch, and internal-hook seams
  - full `pnpm check:changed`; the local Testbox wrapper failed before execution because `crabbox` did not pass its own sanity check

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery

- Revert this PR to remove the new internal hook events and return ACP turn completion to stats-only behavior.
- Existing config and user-facing messaging do not need migration or rollback steps.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook consumers may need either runtime lifecycle completion or durable saved-state readiness, and those are different timing contracts.
  - Mitigation: Split the surface into `agent:turn:end` for lifecycle completion and `agent:turn:save` for persistence outcome reporting, with docs and tests for both.
- Risk: Slow hook handlers could delay turn completion if internal hook execution were awaited.
  - Mitigation: Hook dispatch remains bounded/fire-and-forget via `fireAndForgetBoundedHook()`.

target.title: docs(sms): add Twilio A2P delivery guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a short US A2P / 10DLC delivery section to the SMS channel docs.
- Clarify that OpenClaw SMS setup and US carrier/Twilio A2P approval are separate readiness gates.
- Add troubleshooting guidance for Twilio sends that are accepted by the API but later show `undelivered`, including `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** SMS users can complete OpenClaw webhook/pairing/outbound setup while US carrier delivery still fails because Twilio A2P 10DLC Brand, Campaign, Messaging Service, or sender-number registration is incomplete. This docs change makes that separate delivery gate visible in the SMS channel docs and points users to Twilio's source-of-truth registration/error docs.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `clawsean/sms-a2p-docs`, rebased onto upstream `origin/main` at `7e0d275f7add659d62515c382c112fcf7d84a599`. The edited page is `docs/channels/sms.md`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD -- docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real local checkout after the docs patch:

```text
Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 136ms).
Docs formatting clean (660 files).
checked_internal_links=4544
broken_links=0
```

Also manually reviewed the current Twilio A2P 10DLC overview, registration quickstart, required business/campaign information, and Twilio error pages for `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`. Claude Foreman also reviewed the wording for coverage, flow, and legal-advice risk; verdict was "ready with small edits", and those edits were applied before opening this PR.

**Observed result after fix:** The SMS docs page parses as MDX, passes docs formatting, has no broken internal links, and now contains a concise A2P/10DLC prerequisite section plus a targeted troubleshooting note for Twilio API acceptance followed by carrier delivery failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not send live SMS for this docs-only change. I did not test a Twilio A2P campaign approval flow in the Twilio Console. I did not run full repo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR only changes one docs page and the targeted docs checks above passed.

## AI-assisted

This PR is AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated wording, checked it against Twilio/OpenClaw docs, and applied a second-pass Claude Foreman review before opening.

target.title: [codex] Read exact X posts via FxTwitter
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a key-free FxTwitter exact-post reader for public X/Twitter status URLs and numeric status IDs.
- Route exact post references through FxTwitter before falling back to xAI-backed broader `x_search`.
- Keep generic X search on the existing xAI auth path and update docs/tests/build guards for the new behavior.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw can read an exact public X/Twitter status URL through `x_search` without requiring an xAI API key for that exact post read.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw Gateway/Dashboard, built from this patch, restarted under LaunchAgent, listening on `127.0.0.1:18789` with the promoted runtime snapshot.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built OpenClaw, restarted the Gateway, verified `openclaw gateway status --deep`, then invoked the live Gateway HTTP tool endpoint with `curl` against `x_search` using `https://x.com/jack/status/20`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real Gateway invocation:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "provider": "fxtwitter",
  "mode": "exact_post",
  "statusId": "20",
  "hasExpectedText": true
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Gateway returned `provider=fxtwitter`, `mode=exact_post`, `statusId=20`, and confirmed the expected post text was present (`just setting up my twttr`). This proves the exact-post path used FxTwitter and worked on a real running OpenClaw setup.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for the exact public post-read path. Broader xAI-backed generic X search remains covered by targeted tests and still requires xAI auth.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix: suppress raw provider errors in channel delivery
target.body:
Fixes #69737.

## Summary

This change blocks raw unclassified assistant `errorMessage` text at the user-facing delivery boundary.

It adds a shared `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText(...)` wrapper around the existing assistant error formatter. Classified friendly errors still use the existing sanitizer/classification behavior, but formatter output that is only raw upstream pass-through now falls back to:

```text
LLM request failed.
```

Terminal lifecycle events and reply payload construction now use the safe wrapper for actual assistant error turns. Raw diagnostic details are not globally deleted, provider-side error objects are not changed, and internal observation fields such as `rawErrorPreview` remain available for maintainers.

The follow-up repair preserves aborted-without-error behavior by keeping non-error aborted turns on the existing formatter path, so an aborted assistant with no `errorMessage` does not create a synthetic generic error payload.

## Evidence

- Synthetic canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737`
- Current HEAD tested: `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`
- Raw provider-error suppression remains scoped to `stopReason="error"`.
- Aborted-without-error payload behavior is preserved.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Terminal assistant errors with unclassified raw upstream `errorMessage` must not send that raw text through user-facing lifecycle or reply payload delivery text. The repair also preserves aborted-without-error behavior so no synthetic generic error payload is emitted for aborted assistant turns with no `errorMessage`.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`, running the actual `handleAgentEnd` lifecycle function and actual `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` reply payload builder through `node --import tsx`, plus a live Telegram DM through my existing OpenClaw Telegram bot.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Direct Node/tsx proof imported `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts`, passed a terminal assistant payload with `stopReason: "error"`, `content: []`, and `errorMessage: "SECRET_CANARY_69737"`, then captured user-facing lifecycle event text and reply payload text. For Telegram, the PR checkout was started locally, `openai/gpt-5.5` was temporarily routed to a local OpenAI Responses-compatible endpoint at `127.0.0.1:8787/v1`, and a live Telegram DM triggered a provider 500 containing `SECRET_CANARY_69737`.

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the direct proof command and live Telegram gateway log excerpt:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx direct handleAgentEnd plus buildEmbeddedRunPayloads canary proof",
  "head": "5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f",
  "containsCanaryInUserFacingLifecycleText": false,
  "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingLifecycleText": true,
  "containsCanaryInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": false,
  "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": true,
  "replyPayloads": [
    {
      "text": "LLM request failed.",
      "isError": true
    }
  ],
  "internalRawPreviewRetainedForMaintainers": true
}
```

```text
[openai-transport] [responses] error provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 status=500 code=telegram_live_proof_canary type=server_error message=500 SECRET_CANARY_69737
[telegram] embedded run agent end: isError=true model=gpt-5.5 provider=openai error=LLM request failed. rawError=500 SECRET_CANARY_69737
```

![Live Telegram proof screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f223a2e-76f5-430f-b99c-1be7f2045063)

- Observed result after fix: `SECRET_CANARY_69737` is absent from both user-facing lifecycle text and user-facing reply payload text. The live Telegram transcript displays a generic failure response and does not show `SECRET_CANARY_69737`. Internal diagnostic preview retention still reports true for maintainers. The aborted-without-error regression is covered by the added payload regression test and preserves the no-payload behavior.

- What was not tested: Slack delivery was not exercised. The direct lifecycle/payload behavior and live Telegram DM delivery path were exercised for this PR scope.

## Testing

target.title: feat(trajectory): allow OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override for per-event byte cap
target.body:
## Summary

The per-event byte cap for trajectory recording (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES`) is
hard-coded to 256 KiB in two independent locations:

- `src/trajectory/paths.ts:8` ŌĆö consumed by the core runtime writer
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.ts:35` ŌĆö independent copy in the Codex app-server writer

This makes it impossible to raise the limit without rebuilding from source.
Heavy sessions (large `context.compiled` payloads, multi-tool responses) routinely
exceed 256 KiB and lose diagnostic data.

This PR:

1. Reads `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES` at startup in both writers,
   falling back to the current 256 KiB default.
2. Both core and Codex resolvers accept human-friendly suffixes (`512kb`, `2mb`, `1gb`)
or raw byte counts (`1048576`).
3. Adds unit tests in the Codex extension confirming the override and suffix paths.
4. Documents the new variable in `docs/tools/trajectory.md`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** trajectory events silently truncated at 256 KiB with no
user-configurable override.

**Environment tested:** Linux 6.8.0-106-generic (x64), OpenClaw v2026.5.7 (eeef486),
Node v24.14.1, 1212 trajectory files over 30 days.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l

node -e "const fs=require('fs');const d=fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8');const lines=d.split('
').filter(Boolean);const t=lines.filter(l=>{const o=JSON.parse(l);return o.data?.truncated});console.log('total:',lines.length,'truncated:',t.length)" ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/9e600bba-2bbe-4ff4-98ca-59ea5f1bb896.trajectory.jsonl
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before ŌĆö same production host as ┬¦"Environment tested", current published `openclaw` build, last 30 days of sessions:

```text
$ grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l
459
```

A single heavy session (7.3 MB transcript) had 633 truncated events out of 1891 total ŌĆö 33% data loss under the default 256 KiB cap.

Earlier after-fix driver ŌĆö patched branch head `76877f65f9c42b1154820dba43838ea413b3dc27`, a self-contained driver that wires the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` and records a synthetic oversized payload (60 keys ├Ś 5000 chars = ~293 KB after sanitization, deliberately under the inner `STRING_MAX_CHARS=32768` / `OBJECT_MAX_KEYS=64` / `DEPTH=6` limits so only the outer event-cap fires). The two phases use the **identical payload**; only the env var changes:

```text
==================================================
PHASE ŌĆö default 256 KiB cap, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935876980.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=(unset)
>>> payload: 60 keys ├Ś 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 354
>>> truncated flag: true
>>> truncation originalBytes: 301095
>>> truncation limitBytes:    262144
>>> truncation reason:        trajectory-event-size-limit

==================================================
PHASE ŌĆö OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935877004.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb
>>> payload: 60 keys ├Ś 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 301095
>>> truncated flag: false
>>> payload key count preserved: 60
```

Current head validation ŌĆö patched source checkout at PR head `f6ae71e323f5523e9ecdb894d6990d0ca5639045`, Windows NT 10.0.26100 x64, Node v24.15.0, using the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` path and the same oversized payload shape:

```text
PHASE default-cap
env (unset)
bytes 312
truncated true
reason trajectory-event-size-limit
field_count (truncated sentinel)

PHASE one-mb
env 1mb
bytes 301033
truncated false
reason (none)
field_count 60
```

Current head focused tests also pass after the Windows-portable test expectation fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/trajectory/paths.test.ts src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed | 2 skipped (13)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 19.80s
```

- Phase 1 (no override): the recorded `.trajectory.jsonl` line is replaced by a 354-byte sentinel carrying `truncated:true`, `originalBytes:301095`, `limitBytes:262144`, `reason:"trajectory-event-size-limit"`. This is the current `main` behavior under the default 256 KiB cap.
- Phase 2 (`OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb`): same payload, same recorder. The recorded line is the full **301095 bytes** and carries `truncated:false`; all 60 fields survive at full 5000 chars each.

The byte-for-byte difference (354 ŌåÆ 301095, ~880├Ś) is the direct manifestation of the override raising the per-event cap above the actual payload size. The current core-runtime fix at PR head `f6ae71e323f5523e9ecdb894d6990d0ca5639045` resolves the cap once from `params.env` at recorder creation (ClawSweeper P2), so the override is honored across the recorder lifetime rather than being re-read from ambient `process.env` per event.

The earlier focused vitest set also passed on the previous patch build:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.6

 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the 256 KiB default when env is empty  125ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the default when the override is whitespace only  1ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts raw byte counts  1ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts kb suffixes  0ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts mb suffixes  0ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts gb suffixes  0ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on unparseable input  0ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on non-positive input  0ms
 Ō£ō unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > module-load constant matches the empty-env resolver result  0ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > keeps write flags usable when O_NOFOLLOW is unavailable  9ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > records by default unless explicitly disabled  5ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > sanitizes session ids when resolving an override directory  3ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors explicit disablement  1ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > refuses to append through a symlinked parent directory  1ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override  3ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > truncates events that exceed the runtime event byte limit  4ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > accepts human-friendly byte-size suffixes for the event cap override  2ms
 Ō£ō extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > falls back to default on invalid suffix values  2ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  16.93s
```

The new test case `honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override` directly exercises the env-injection path at recorder creation.

**Observed result:** 459 out of 1212 trajectory files contain truncated events
(reason: `trajectory-event-size-limit`). A single heavy session
(`9e600bba`) has 633 truncated events out of 1891 total ŌĆö 33% data loss.
After the patch, setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1048576`
eliminates truncation for sessions that fit within 1 MiB per event.
Setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=2mb` is equivalent.

**Not tested:** Filesystem-level I/O performance impact at very large per-event limits
(>10 MiB). The file-level cap (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_FILE_MAX_BYTES = 50 MiB`) still
applies and prevents unbounded disk growth.
## Addressing ClawSweeper review

### P2 ŌĆö Resolve the core cap from the recorder env (`src/trajectory/runtime.ts:121`)

Fixed. `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` already captured `params.env` for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` enablement and trajectory path resolution; the truncation path was re-reading the per-event byte cap from ambient `process.env` on every event, which (a) silently ignored an injected `params.env` override and (b) let mid-session ambient mutations change behavior.

The truncation path now resolves the cap **once** at recorder creation from the captured `env`:

```ts
// inside createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder, after `env` is resolved
const eventMaxBytes = resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes(env);

// later, in buildEventLine
const boundedLine = truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent(event, line, eventMaxBytes);
```

`truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent` no longer calls `resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes()` internally; it accepts the pre-resolved `limitBytes` as a parameter. This matches the pinning semantics already used for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` and `resolveTrajectoryFilePath`.

### Prior review items (resolved earlier on this branch)

1. **F1 ŌĆö broad SDK barrel import**: reverted to narrow subpaths.

   ```ts
   import { resolveUserPath } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime";
   import { appendRegularFile, resolveRegularFileAppendFlags } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime";
   ```

2. **F2 ŌĆö re-implemented file-write safety**: restored shared helpers. The hot append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and `resolveRegularFileAppendFlags()` from `security-runtime` (no local symlink/hardlink/nlink rewrite for the append path).

3. **F3 ŌĆö invalid-fallback test using a valid value**: now uses a truly malformed value.

   ```ts
   env: { OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES: "abc-not-a-size" },
   ```

4. **F4 ŌĆö CHANGELOG.md conflict**: rebased on latest `main`.

### Note on `lstatSync` calls in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`

Two `lstatSync(...).isSymbolicLink()` checks remain in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`. They are **not** related to the F2 finding:

- They guard the **trajectory pointer file** (`*.trajectory-path.json`), a separate sidecar JSON file written via `openSync` + `writeFileSync` to an attacker-untrusted path.
- The trajectory main file (`*.trajectory.jsonl`) append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and is unaffected.
- These pointer-specific symlink checks were added on this PR (no equivalent in current `main`) to harden the pointer write path. They are independent of the per-event byte cap feature, but are kept because removing them would lose the symlink protection on the pointer file.

If reviewers prefer the pointer-write symlink hardening to land in a follow-up PR rather than this one, happy to move them out ŌĆö please advise.

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; PR head `55811ad9edce27d49decb99c7564fba5c4138fb3`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm docs:list` - `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/channel-plugin-resolution.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/channels.add.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm build` - `pnpm check:changed` - Evidence after fix: `src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts` passes 16/16 after exposing `listRawChannelPluginCatalogEntries` through the same mock/actual fallback as the old catalog helper; the focused loader/registry/catalog/channel setup tests pass after the rebase; `pnpm build` and the final `pnpm check:changed` pass; `CHANGELOG.md` and stale transcripts tool-display metadata are no longer in the final branch diff. - Observed result after fix: disabled workspace shadows no longer execute through scoped setup-only loads; trusted workspace overrides remain available; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected as a registry backstop; malformed trusted setup-entry channel registration records a diagnostic and sibling plugins keep loading. - What was not tested: live channel setup against a real external workspace plugin outside the loader test harness. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same minimal setup-only repro failed on latest `origin/main` before this branch because the marker file was created during scoped setup-only load. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: the scoped setup-only loader path treated explicit `onlyPluginIds` as sufficient to load a disabled workspace channel plugin, so workspace trust was bypassed below the trusted-catalog call sites. - Missing detection / guardrail: registry assembly still accepted channel registration from disabled workspace plugins if a lower-level caller imported one. - Complementary #85774 root cause: the setup-entry loader loop caught surrounding registration failures, but `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)` itself was unguarded, so one throwing trusted channel registration could abort sibling plugin loading. - Contributing context: latest `main` already absorbed the higher-level `channels add` / trusted-catalog call-site fixes from #64154, but not this lower-level loader/registry backstop or the setup-entry error-isolation guard. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts`, `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`, and existing channel setup/add tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: explicit scoped setup-only loads must reject disabled workspace channel plugins but keep trusted workspace channel plugins available; registry assembly must reject disabled workspace channel registrations; trusted setup-entry registration errors must be recorded without aborting healthy sibling plugins. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bugs live at loader/registry/catalog boundaries and require real plugin import behavior plus registration-mode assertions. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `only setup-loads a disabled channel plugin when the caller scopes to the selected plugin` still covers the non-workspace path. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Untrusted workspace channel shadows no longer execute during setup-only scoped loads. - Trusted workspace channel plugins remain available to setup-time scoped loading. - A broken trusted setup-entry channel registration now surfaces as a plugin diagnostic instead of stopping sibling plugin loading. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: Node 22 local repo checkout - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader / channel plugin registry / channel setup catalog - Relevant config (redacted): workspace plugins enabled; untrusted plugin omitted from allowlist ### Steps 1. Create a workspace channel plugin fixture with `channels` metadata and a side-effect marker write in `register()`. 2. Load it through `loadOpenClawPlugins({ includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, forceSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, onlyPluginIds: [...] })`. 3. Compare disabled workspace behavior against trusted workspace behavior. 4. Load a setup-entry channel whose channel config getter throws during `registerChannel` alongside a healthy sibling plugin. ### Expected - Disabled workspace channel plugin stays disabled and does not execute. - Trusted workspace channel plugin remains available to setup-only loading. - A throwing trusted setup-entry channel records a diagnostic and does not prevent sibling plugins from loading. ### Actual - This branch matches the expected behavior. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets in test output - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: untrusted/trusted workspace scoped setup-only regressions, disabled workspace registry guard, trusted-catalog filtering semantics, onboarding channel setup e2e catalog mock compatibility, `channels add` workspace shadow fallback, #85774 setup-entry `registerChannel` error isolation, `pnpm build`, and final `pnpm check:changed`. - Edge cases checked: trusted workspace override still loads in setup-only mode; non-workspace disabled plugin behavior stays unchanged; setup wizard discovery still keeps workspace-only install candidates visible without treating them as installed/executable. - What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end channel setup outside the loader test harness. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) No. This is an intentional security hardening break for disabled workspace channel plugins that were previously reachable through setup-only scoped loads. - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No automatic migration. Operators who intentionally develop or run a workspace/linked channel plugin must explicitly enable/trust that plugin before setup-time code can run. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: use the normal plugin enable/trust flow for the workspace plugin before running channel setup. The behavior is documented in `docs/cli/plugins.md`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a trusted workspace plugin could accidentally get blocked if the trust check were too broad. - Mitigation: regression coverage explicitly keeps the trusted workspace scoped-load path green. - Risk: setup discovery could accidentally hide workspace-only install candidates. - Mitigation: trusted-catalog and discovery tests keep the strict execution list separate from the lenient setup wizard display list. - Risk: an existing disabled workspace channel plugin that relied on setup-only execution now fails closed. - Mitigation: this is the intended security behavior; the docs now state that workspace-origin plugins are not imported or executed until explicitly enabled/trusted. ### Proof 1: Pre-Fix Reproduction on origin/main - Behavior addressed: Untrusted workspace channel plugin (evi1-channel-probe) executes its register() function via the setup-only scoped load path when includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true is passed to loadOpenClawPlugins, bypassing the trust boundary. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=391883b7652bcb464c523f64ddcabb9bcad47f92e434737b7cc5391f7bc0922f, run=cbx_6dadb5d505eb, checkout SHA=ead847f6065ac36031f9e7a95dd1ee90f171f79c (origin/main) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch --depth=1 origin main && git checkout FETCH_HEAD` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) Deploy evi1-channel-probe plugin to /tmp/proof1-workspace/.openclaw/extensions/evi1-channel-probe/; (4) Confirm /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence absent; (5) Run `node probe-untrusted.mjs` with onlyPluginIds=["evi1-channel-probe"], includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins=true, activate=false, cache=false; (6) Check /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence existence. - Evidence after fix: Marker file /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence CREATED with content "evi1-probe-executed\n". Plugin status="disabled" with channelSetups=[{id:"evi1-channel-probe"}] ŌĆö the setup-only path imported and executed the untrusted workspace plugin code. - Observed result after fix: On origin/main (pre-fix), the untrusted workspace channel plugin code ran despite being disabled. The plugin appeared in channelSetups (not channels), status was "disabled", yet register() executed and created the marker file. This confirms the bypass vulnerability. - What was not tested: CLI blackbox path, real channels add flow ### Proof 2: Post-Fix Verification on PR Head - Behavior addressed: The PR fix blocks untrusted workspace channel plugins from executing during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected at registry assembly; trusted workspace plugins with plugins.allow still work; broken trusted setup-entry registerChannel records a diagnostic without aborting sibling plugins. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=df29879e2faef7ac8794cba519c1988aff410316a3718d90c6f638cd9977ce15, lease=cbx_0f687f5da081, checkout SHA=b05a7b14fe9bd6c119b3b7fe60a1967d0987804c (PR head at time of test; refreshed #86953 head is `2a9a2d79fd498be5fc62e83634a544f873b67b4c`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch origin pull/82579/head:pr-head && git checkout pr-head` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) `grep -r "channel registration rejected for disabled workspace plugin" dist/` ŌĆö fix code found in dist/loader-DM-dphfh.js; (4) Deploy evi1-channel-probe (untrusted), confirm marker ABSENT after probe; (5) Deploy evi1-trusted-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm marker EXISTS; (6) Deploy evi1-broken-register + evi1-sibling-probe (with plugins.allow), confirm sibling marker EXISTS and diagnostic recorded; (7) Run focused tests: `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts` (142 pass), `pnpm test src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts` (2 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts` (5 pass), `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts` (6 pass); (8) `pnpm check:changed` ŌĆö 0 errors. - Evidence after fix: Untrusted probe: MARKER_ABSENT, exit code 0 (clean block, not crash). Plugin status="disabled", channelSetups=[]. Trusted probe: MARKER_EXISTS, content "evi1-probe-executed\n". Plugin status="loaded", channels and channelSetups populated. Error isolation: broken-register records diagnostic (level=error, pluginId=evi1-broken-register), process exit code 0. Sibling marker EXISTS, content "evi1-sibling-probe-executed\n". - Observed result after fix: The fix blocks untrusted workspace channel plugin execution. ## Summary

- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads;
